title: Caesar salad
subtitle: Classic American salad with anchovy-parmesan dressing and croutons.
excerpt: Cos lettuce tossed in a deeply savoury dressing of anchovies, garlic, parmesan, egg yolk and lemon, with golden croutons made in the pan. Built from scratch, not a bottle. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Caesar salad. The dish was created by Caesar Cardini, an Italian-born restaurant owner in Tijuana, Mexico, in 1924. The original used whole leaves, a coddled egg dressing, parmesan and croutons — anchovies were not in the original but became standard in later versions. The recipe is documented in American culinary literature from the 1930s onwards and became one of the most popular salads in the world.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The dressing can be made 2 days ahead and refrigerated. Dress the salad just before serving.

content.text: A proper Caesar dressing is made from scratch, not from a bottle. The anchovies dissolve into the dressing and provide saltiness and depth rather than a fishy taste. Mashing them to a paste with the garlic before adding any liquid is the key step. Worcestershire sauce is not in the original recipe but is now standard and correct.

items.prepNote: cut into 1 cm cubes for croutons
finely grated, plus extra to serve

content.text: Make the croutons and the dressing

Heat {{rapeseed-oil}} of oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Add {{bread}} of bread cubes and fry for 5 to 6 minutes, turning regularly, until golden on all sides. Drain on paper and season. For the dressing, mash {{anchovies}} of anchovy fillets and {{garlic}} of garlic clove to a paste with the flat of a knife. Combine in a bowl with {{eggs}} egg yolk, the juice of {{lemon}} lemon and {{worcestershire-sauce}} of Worcestershire sauce. Whisk in {{olive-oil}} of olive oil in a thin, steady stream until emulsified. Stir in half the {{parmesan}} of parmesan. Season with {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt and {{black-pepper}} of pepper.
Toss the torn cos lettuce leaves with the dressing in a large bowl until well coated. Add the croutons and remaining parmesan. Serve immediately; the salad wilts quickly once dressed.

items.cause: Too much lemon, or anchovies' salt not accounted for.
items.fix: Taste before seasoning; the anchovies are salty. Add lemon gradually and balance with a little more oil if needed.
Pour the oil very slowly in a thin stream while whisking constantly. If it splits, start with a fresh yolk and whisk the split dressing into it very slowly.

items.cause: Added too early, or pan not hot enough.
items.fix: Fry croutons until properly golden and crisp. Add to the dressed salad only just before serving.

content.text: Caesar salad began as a borderland dish, created in Tijuana by an Italian immigrant for American tourists, and became a global restaurant standard. The dressing is one of the great balancing acts in cooking: fat, salt, acid and umami in equilibrium. It is also the best argument for not buying a bottled dressing. Making it from scratch takes ten minutes and produces a result in a different category.
